questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在将其应用程序和数据库迁移到 AWS 云。该公司将使用 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS)、AWS Direct Connect 和 Amazon RDS。哪些活动将由公司的运营团队管理？ （选择三项。）
question_text.en: A company is migrating its applications and databases to the AWS Cloud. The company will use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS), AWS Direct Connect, and Amazon RDS. Which activities will be managed by the company's operational team? (Choose three.)

A.zh: Amazon RDS 基础设施层、操作系统和平台的管理
A.en: Management of the Amazon RDS infrastructure layer, operating system, and platforms

B.zh: 创建 Amazon RDS 数据库实例并配置计划维护时段
B.en: Creation of an Amazon RDS DB instance and configuring the scheduled maintenance window

C.zh: 在 Amazon ECS 上配置其他软件组件，用于监控、补丁管理、日志管理和主机入侵检测
C.en: Configuration of additional software components on Amazon ECS for monitoring, patch management, log management, and host intrusion detection

D.zh: 为 Amazon RDS 的所有次要和主要数据库版本安装补丁
D.en: Installation of patches for all minor and major database versions for Amazon RDS

E.zh: 确保数据中心内 Amazon RDS 基础设施的物理安全
E.en: Ensure the physical security of the Amazon RDS infrastructure in the data center

F.zh: 对通过 Direct Connect 传输的数据进行加密
F.en: Encryption of the data that moves in transit through Direct Connect

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：管理 Amazon RDS 基础设施层、操作系统和平台错误。根据AWS的责任共担模型，Amazon RDS的基础设施层、操作系统和平台都由AWS管理，用户不需要管理这些部分。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Management of the Amazon RDS infrastructure layer, operating system, and platforms errors. According to AWS's Shared Responsibility Model, Amazon RDS's infrastructure layer, operating system, and platform are managed by AWS, and users do not need to manage these parts.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon RDS 数据库实例的创建和计划维护时段的配置正确。创建 Amazon RDS 数据库实例并配置计划维护时段是用户的操作责任。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Creation of an Amazon RDS DB instance and configuring the scheduled maintenance window is correct. It is the user's operational responsibility to create an Amazon RDS database instance and configure scheduled maintenance windows.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：管理、主机入侵检测正确。用户需要在Amazon ECS上配置额外的软件组件，例如监控、补丁管理、日志管理和主机入侵检测，这些都是用户的责任。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: management, and host intrusion detection are correct. Users need to configure additional software components on Amazon ECS, such as monitoring, patch management, log management, and host intrusion detection, which are the user's responsibility.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：为所有次要和主要数据库版本安装 Amazon RDS 错误补丁。 AWS 负责修补 Amazon RDS 的所有档案和主数据库样本，用户不需要管理这些部分。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Installation of patches for all minor and major database versions for Amazon RDS error. AWS is responsible for patching all archives and main database specimens for Amazon RDS, and users do not need to manage these parts.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：确保数据中心内Amazon RDS基础设施的物理安全性。 AWS负责数据中心的管理和安全，用户不需要管理这些部分。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Ensure the physical security of the Amazon RDS infrastructure in the data center error. AWS is responsible for the management and security of the data center, and users do not need to manage these parts.

F.zh: 正確。技术原因：通过 Direct Connect 传输的数据的加密是正确的。用户有责任对通过 Direct Connect 传输的数据进行加密。
F.en: Correct. Technical reason: Encryption of the data that moves in transit through Direct Connect is correct. It is the user's responsibility to encrypt the data in transmission via Direct Connect.

answer_text.zh: B. 创建 Amazon RDS 数据库实例并配置计划维护时段；C. 在 Amazon ECS 上配置其他软件组件，用于监控、补丁管理、日志管理和主机入侵检测；F. 对通过 Direct Connect 传输的数据进行加密
answer_text.en: B. Creation of an Amazon RDS DB instance and configuring the scheduled maintenance window; C. Configuration of additional software components on Amazon ECS for monitoring, patch management, log management, and host intrusion detection; F. Encryption of the data that moves in transit through Direct Connect

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BCF (98%) 2%。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon RDS 数据库实例的创建和计划维护时段的配置正确。创建 Amazon RDS 数据库实例并配置计划维护时段是用户的操作责任。；管理、主机入侵检测正确。用户需要在Amazon ECS上配置额外的软件组件，例如监控、补丁管理、日志管理和主机入侵检测，这些都是用户的责任。；通过 Direct Connect 传输的数据的加密是正确的。用户有责任对通过 Direct Connect 传输的数据进行加密。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BCF (98%) 2%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Creation of an Amazon RDS DB instance and configuring the scheduled maintenance window is correct. It is the user's operational responsibility to create an Amazon RDS database instance and configure scheduled maintenance windows.; management, and host intrusion detection are correct. Users need to configure additional software components on Amazon ECS, such as monitoring, patch management, log management, and host intrusion detection, which are the user's responsibility.; Encryption of the data that moves in transit through Direct Connect is correct. It is the user's responsibility to encrypt the data in transmission via Direct Connect.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 Amazon EC2 实例上运行基于 Java 的作业。该作业每小时运行一次，运行时间为 10 秒。该作业按计划的时间间隔运行并消耗 1 GB 内存。除了作业使用最大可用 CPU 的短暂激增之外，实例的 CPU 利用率较低。该公司希望优化这项工作的成本。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a Java-based job on an Amazon EC2 instance. The job runs every hour and takes 10 seconds to run. The job runs on a scheduled interval and consumes 1 GB of memory. The CPU utilization of the instance is low except for short surges during which the job uses the maximum CPU available. The company wants to optimize the costs to run the job. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS App2Container (A2C) 对作业进行容器化。在具有 0.5 个虚拟 CPU (vCPU) 和 1 GB 内存的 AWS Fargate 上将作业作为 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 任务运行。
A.en: Use AWS App2Container (A2C) to containerize the job. Run the job as an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) task on AWS Fargate with 0.5 virtual CPU (vCPU) and 1 GB of memory.

B.zh: 将代码复制到具有 1 GB 内存的 AWS Lambda 函数中。创建 Amazon EventBridge 计划规则以每小时运行代码。
B.en: Copy the code into an AWS Lambda function that has 1 GB of memory. Create an Amazon EventBridge scheduled rule to run the code each hour.

C.zh: 使用 AWS App2Container (A2C) 对作业进行容器化。在现有的 Amazon 系统映像 (AMI) 中安装容器。确保计划在任务完成时停止容器。
C.en: Use AWS App2Container (A2C) to containerize the job. Install the container in the existing Amazon Machine Image (AMI). Ensure that the schedule stops the container when the task finishes.

D.zh: 配置现有计划以在作业完成时停止 EC2 实例，并在下一个作业开始时重新启动 EC2 实例。
D.en: Configure the existing schedule to stop the EC2 instance at the completion of the job and restart the EC2 instance when the next job starts.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然任务可以运行，但与 AWS Lambda 相比，Fargate 的成本优化并不理想，任务启动时间可能会更长。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although tasks can be run, Fargate's cost optimization is not ideal compared to AWS Lambda, and task startup time may be longer.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：Lambda的启动时间极短，并且根据使用时间计费。非常适合运行10秒的任务，成本最低。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Lambda's startup time is extremely short and it is billed based on usage time. It is very suitable for running tasks of 10 seconds and has the lowest cost.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：与AWS Lambda相比，这种方法更加复杂，并且无法实现最优的成本优化。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Compared with AWS Lambda, this method is more complex and cannot achieve optimal cost optimization.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：EC2实例启动时间长，管理复杂度高。直接运行任务效率不如AWS Lambda。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The startup time of EC2 instances is long and the management complexity is high. It is not as efficient as AWS Lambda to run tasks directly.

answer_text.zh: B. 将代码复制到具有 1 GB 内存的 AWS Lambda 函数中。创建 Amazon EventBridge 计划规则以每小时运行代码。
answer_text.en: B. Copy the code into an AWS Lambda function that has 1 GB of memory. Create an Amazon EventBridge scheduled rule to run the code each hour.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Lambda的启动时间极短，并且根据使用时间计费。非常适合运行10秒的任务，成本最低。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Lambda's startup time is extremely short and it is billed based on usage time. It is very suitable for running tasks of 10 seconds and has the lowest cost.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司想要为 Amazon EC2 数据和多个 Amazon S3 存储桶实施备份策略。由于法规要求，公司必须将备份文件保留特定时间段。在保留期内，公司不得更改文件。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company wants to implement a backup strategy for Amazon EC2 data and multiple Amazon S3 buckets. Because of regulatory requirements, the company must retain backup files for a specific time period. The company must not alter the files for the duration of the retention period. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Backup 创建在治理模式下具有保管库锁的备份保管库。创建所需的备份计划。
A.en: Use AWS Backup to create a backup vault that has a vault lock in governance mode. Create the required backup plan.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager 创建所需的自动快照策略。
B.en: Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager to create the required automated snapshot policy.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon S3 文件网关创建备份。配置适当的 S3 生命周期管理。
C.en: Use Amazon S3 File Gateway to create the backup. Configure the appropriate S3 Lifecycle management.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Backup 创建在合规模式下具有保管库锁的备份保管库。创建所需的备份计划。
D.en: Use AWS Backup to create a backup vault that has a vault lock in compliance mode. Create the required backup plan.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：治理模式允许用户在锁定后仍然可以修改或删除具有特定权限的备份文件。因为标题要求“文件不得更改”，管理模式不符合要求。因此这个选项是错误的。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Governance Mode allows users to still modify or delete backup files with specific permissions after being locked. Because the title requires "the file must not be changed", the management model does not meet the requirements. Therefore this option is wrong.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager主要用于管理EC2实例的快照生命周期，但无法满足S3数据的备份需求。此外，它不提供文件不可变的能力。因此这个选项是错误的。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager is mainly used to manage the snapshot life cycle of EC2 instances, but it cannot meet the backup needs of S3 data. Additionally, it does not provide the ability for files to be immutable. Therefore this option is wrong.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon S3 文件网关主要用于将本地文件存储到S3，但不提供文件不可更改的备份功能。题目要求“文件不能被改变”，这个选项不能满足要求。因此这个选项是错误的。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon S3 File Gateway is mainly used to store local files to S3, but it does not provide the function of backing up files unchangeable. The question requires "the file must not be changed", and this option cannot meet the requirement. Therefore this option is wrong.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：合规模式保证备份文件被锁定后无法修改或删除，完全满足问题的要求。 AWS Backup支持EC2和S3的备份，可以满足问题中两种资源的备份需求。因此这个选项是正确的。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: Compliance Mode ensures that backup files cannot be modified or deleted after being locked, which fully meets the requirements of the question. AWS Backup supports the backup of EC2 and S3, which can meet the backup requirements of the two resources in the question. Therefore this option is correct.

answer_text.zh: D. 使用 AWS Backup 创建在合规模式下具有保管库锁的备份保管库。创建所需的备份计划。
answer_text.en: D. Use AWS Backup to create a backup vault that has a vault lock in compliance mode. Create the required backup plan.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：D (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：合规模式保证备份文件被锁定后无法修改或删除，完全满足问题的要求。 AWS Backup支持EC2和S3的备份，可以满足问题中两种资源的备份需求。因此这个选项是正确的。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Compliance Mode ensures that backup files cannot be modified or deleted after being locked, which fully meets the requirements of the question. AWS Backup supports the backup of EC2 and S3, which can meet the backup requirements of the two resources in the question. Therefore this option is correct.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司拥有跨多个 AWS 区域和账户的资源。新雇用的解决方案架构师发现以前的员工没有提供有关资源库存的详细信息。解决方案架构师需要构建和映射所有账户中各种工作负载的关系详细信息。哪种解决方案能够以最高效的方式满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company has resources across multiple AWS Regions and accounts. A newly hired solutions architect discovers a previous employee did not provide details about the resources inventory. The solutions architect needs to build and map the relationship details of the various workloads across all accounts. Which solution will meet these requirements in the MOST operationally efficient way?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Systems Manager Inventory 从详细视图报告生成地图视图。
A.en: Use AWS Systems Manager Inventory to generate a map view from the detailed view report.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Step Functions 收集工作负载详细信息。手动构建工作负载的架构图。
B.en: Use AWS Step Functions to collect workload details. Build architecture diagrams of the workloads manually.

C.zh: 使用 AWS 上的工作负载发现生成工作负载的架构图。
C.en: Use Workload Discovery on AWS to generate architecture diagrams of the workloads.

D.zh: 使用 AWS X-Ray 查看工作负载详细信息。构建具有关系的架构图。
D.en: Use AWS X-Ray to view the workload details. Build architecture diagrams with relationships.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Systems Manager Inventory 主要用于收集和管理实例及其关联资源的详细信息，但它不具备直接生成架构图或跨账户、跨区域资源关系图的能力。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Systems Manager Inventory is mainly used to collect and manage detailed information about instances and their associated resources, but it does not have the ability to directly generate architecture diagrams or cross-account and cross-region resource relationship diagrams.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Step Functions 是一种用于构建工作流程的编排服务。虽然它可以用来收集工作负载信息，但手动构建架构图效率低下，并且不能满足问题所要求的“操作上最高效”的解决方案。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Step Functions is an orchestration service for building workflows. Although it can be used to collect workload information, manually building architecture diagrams is inefficient and does not meet the "most operationally efficient" solution required by the question.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS上的Workload Discovery是一个可以自动发现资源并生成架构图的工具。支持跨账户、跨地域的资源映射，非常适合题主的要求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Workload Discovery on AWS is a tool that can automatically discover resources and generate architecture diagrams. It supports cross-account and cross-region resource mapping, which is very suitable for the requirements of the question.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS X-Ray主要用于分布式应用程序的性能分析和跟踪，不适合生成跨账户、跨区域的架构图。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS X-Ray is mainly used for performance analysis and tracking of distributed application programs, and is not suitable for generating cross-account and cross-region architecture diagrams.

answer_text.zh: C. 使用 AWS 上的工作负载发现生成工作负载的架构图。
answer_text.en: C. Use Workload Discovery on AWS to generate architecture diagrams of the workloads.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (97%) 3%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS上的Workload Discovery是一个可以自动发现资源并生成架构图的工具。支持跨账户、跨地域的资源映射，非常适合题主的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (97%) 3%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Workload Discovery on AWS is a tool that can automatically discover resources and generate architecture diagrams. It supports cross-account and cross-region resource mapping, which is very suitable for the requirements of the question.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 AWS Organizations。该公司希望以不同的预算来运营其一些 AWS 账户。该公司希望在特定时间段内达到分配的预算阈值时接收警报并自动阻止在 AWS 账户上配置额外资源。哪种解决方案组合可以满足这些要求？ （选择三项。）
question_text.en: A company uses AWS Organizations. The company wants to operate some of its AWS accounts with different budgets. The company wants to receive alerts and automatically prevent provisioning of additional resources on AWS accounts when the allocated budget threshold is met during a specific period. Which combination of solutions will meet these requirements? (Choose three.)

A.zh: 使用 AWS Budgets 创建预算。在所需 AWS 账户的成本和使用情况报告部分下设置预算金额。
A.en: Use AWS Budgets to create a budget. Set the budget amount under the Cost and Usage Reports section of the required AWS accounts.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Budgets 创建预算。在所需 AWS 账户的账单仪表板下设置预算金额。
B.en: Use AWS Budgets to create a budget. Set the budget amount under the Billing dashboards of the required AWS accounts.

C.zh: 为 AWS Budgets 创建 IAM 用户以使用所需权限运行预算操作。
C.en: Create an IAM user for AWS Budgets to run budget actions with the required permissions.

D.zh: 为 AWS Budgets 创建 IAM 角色，以使用所需权限运行预算操作。
D.en: Create an IAM role for AWS Budgets to run budget actions with the required permissions.

E.zh: 添加警报，以便在每个账户达到其预算阈值时通知公司。添加预算操作，选择使用适当的配置规则创建的 IAM 身份，以防止配置额外资源。
E.en: Add an alert to notify the company when each account meets its budget threshold. Add a budget action that selects the IAM identity created with the appropriate config rule to prevent provisioning of additional resources.

F.zh: 添加警报，以便在每个账户达到其预算阈值时通知公司。添加预算操作，选择使用适当的服务控制策略 (SCP) 创建的 IAM 身份，以防止配置额外资源。
F.en: Add an alert to notify the company when each account meets its budget threshold. Add a budget action that selects the IAM identity created with the appropriate service control policy (SCP) to prevent provisioning of additional resources.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Budgets 的预算设置不在“成本和使用情况报告”部分，而是在账单仪表板中。该选项的描述不准确。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The budget setting of AWS Budgets is not in the "Cost and Usage Reports" section, but in the Billing Dashboard. This option is described inaccurately.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS 预算的预算创建和设置是在账单控制面板中完成的，并且此选项的描述正确。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Budget creation and setup for AWS Budgets is done in the Billing Dashboard and this option is described correctly.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Budgets 通常使用 IAM 角色而不是 IAM 用户来执行预算操作。 IAM角色更适合跨账户或服务的权限管理。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Budgets typically uses IAM roles rather than IAM users to perform budget operations. IAM roles are more suitable for permission management across accounts or services.

D.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Budgets 需要 IAM 角色来执行预算操作，例如触发警报或限制资源创建。该选项的描述正确。
D.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Budgets requires an IAM role to perform budget operations, such as triggering alerts or limiting resource creation. This option is described correctly.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：预算操作通常使用 SCP（服务控制策略）来限制资源创建，而不是通过 Config 规则。该选项的描述不准确。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Budget operations typically use SCP (Service Control Policy) to limit resource creation rather than through Config rules. This option is described inaccurately.

F.zh: 正確。技术原因：SCP 是 AWS Organizations 中的一个有效工具，用于限制账户行为，防止资源创建超出预算。该选项的描述正确。
F.en: Correct. Technical reason: SCP is an effective tool in AWS Organizations for limiting account behavior, preventing resource creation beyond budget. This option is described correctly.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 AWS Budgets 创建预算。在所需 AWS 账户的账单仪表板下设置预算金额。；D. 为 AWS Budgets 创建 IAM 角色，以使用所需权限运行预算操作。；F. 添加警报，以便在每个账户达到其预算阈值时通知公司。添加预算操作，选择使用适当的服务控制策略 (SCP) 创建的 IAM 身份，以防止配置额外资源。
answer_text.en: B. Use AWS Budgets to create a budget. Set the budget amount under the Billing dashboards of the required AWS accounts.; D. Create an IAM role for AWS Budgets to run budget actions with the required permissions.; F. Add an alert to notify the company when each account meets its budget threshold. Add a budget action that selects the IAM identity created with the appropriate service control policy (SCP) to prevent provisioning of additional resources.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BDF (71%) ADF (15%) 12%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS 预算的预算创建和设置是在账单控制面板中完成的，并且此选项的描述正确。；AWS Budgets 需要 IAM 角色来执行预算操作，例如触发警报或限制资源创建。该选项的描述正确。；SCP 是 AWS Organizations 中的一个有效工具，用于限制账户行为，防止资源创建超出预算。该选项的描述正确。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BDF (71%) ADF (15%) 12%. Popular discussion and key point summary: Budget creation and setup for AWS Budgets is done in the Billing Dashboard and this option is described correctly.; AWS Budgets requires an IAM role to perform budget operations, such as triggering alerts or limiting resource creation. This option is described correctly.; SCP is an effective tool in AWS Organizations for limiting account behavior, preventing resource creation beyond budget. This option is described correctly.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在一个 AWS 区域的 Amazon EC2 实例上运行应用程序。该公司希望将 EC2 实例备份到第二个区域。该公司还希望在第二个区域配置 EC2 资源，并从一个 AWS 账户集中管理 EC2 实例。哪种解决方案能够最具成本效益地满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs applications on Amazon EC2 instances in one AWS Region. The company wants to back up the EC2 instances to a second Region. The company also wants to provision EC2 resources in the second Region and manage the EC2 instances centrally from one AWS account. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 创建在第二个区域中具有相似数量 EC2 实例的灾难恢复 (DR) 计划。配置数据复制。
A.en: Create a disaster recovery (DR) plan that has a similar number of EC2 instances in the second Region. Configure data replication.

B.zh: 创建 EC2 实例的时间点 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 快照。定期将快照复制到第二个区域。
B.en: Create point-in-time Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) snapshots of the EC2 instances. Copy the snapshots to the second Region periodically.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Backup 创建备份计划。为 EC2 实例配置到第二个区域的跨区域备份。
C.en: Create a backup plan by using AWS Backup. Configure cross-Region backup to the second Region for the EC2 instances.

D.zh: 在第二个区域中部署类似数量的 EC2 实例。使用 AWS DataSync 将数据从源区域传输到第二个区域。
D.en: Deploy a similar number of EC2 instances in the second Region. Use AWS DataSync to transfer the data from the source Region to the second Region.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：由于需要持续运行额外的 EC2 实例，直接在第二个区域部署类似数量的 EC2 实例并配置数据复制将导致高昂的成本。不符合“最具成本效益”的要求。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Deploying a similar number of EC2 instances directly in a second region and configuring data replication would result in high costs due to the need to continuously run additional EC2 instances. Does not meet the "most cost effective" requirement.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：该解决方案无法同时满足问题中的关键性能、可用性、安全性、成本或运维约束，因此不是最佳选择。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: This solution cannot simultaneously meet the key performance, availability, security, cost or operation and maintenance constraints in the question, so it is not the best choice.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Backup 支持自动备份计划，并可以配置跨区域备份。提供集中管理能力，满足“最具成本效益”的要求。这是最好的解决方案。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Backup supports automated backup plans and can configure cross-region backups. Provides centralized management capabilities to meet "most cost-effective" requirements. This is the best solution.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS DataSync 主要用于文件数据传输，而不是 EC2 实例的备份。此外，部署类似数量的 EC2 实例会导致成本高昂，无法满足“最具成本效益”的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS DataSync is primarily used for file data transfer, not backup of EC2 instances. Additionally, deploying a similar number of EC2 instances would result in high costs and would not meet the "most cost-effective" requirement.

answer_text.zh: C. 使用 AWS Backup 创建备份计划。为 EC2 实例配置到第二个区域的跨区域备份。
answer_text.en: C. Create a backup plan by using AWS Backup. Configure cross-Region backup to the second Region for the EC2 instances.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (63%) B (25%) 13%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Backup 支持自动备份计划，并可以配置跨区域备份。提供集中管理能力，满足“最具成本效益”的要求。这是最好的解决方案。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (63%) B (25%) 13%. Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Backup supports automated backup plans and can configure cross-region backups. Provides centralized management capabilities to meet "most cost-effective" requirements. This is the best solution.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家使用 AWS 的公司正在构建一个应用程序来将数据传输给产品制造商。该公司拥有自己的身份提供商 (IdP)。该公司希望 IdP 在用户使用应用程序传输数据时对应用程序用户进行身份验证。公司必须使用适用性声明 2 (AS2) 协议。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company that uses AWS is building an application to transfer data to a product manufacturer. The company has its own identity provider (IdP). The company wants the IdP to authenticate application users while the users use the application to transfer data. The company must use Applicability Statement 2 (AS2) protocol. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS DataSync 传输数据。创建用于 IdP 身份验证的 AWS Lambda 函数。
A.en: Use AWS DataSync to transfer the data. Create an AWS Lambda function for IdP authentication.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon AppFlow 流传输数据。创建用于 IdP 身份验证的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 任务。
B.en: Use Amazon AppFlow flows to transfer the data. Create an Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) task for IdP authentication.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Transfer Family 传输数据。创建用于 IdP 身份验证的 AWS Lambda 函数。
C.en: Use AWS Transfer Family to transfer the data. Create an AWS Lambda function for IdP authentication.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Storage Gateway 传输数据。创建用于 IdP 身份验证的 Amazon Cognito 身份池。
D.en: Use AWS Storage Gateway to transfer the data. Create an Amazon Cognito identity pool for IdP authentication.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS DataSync主要用于在不同存储系统之间高效传输数据，但不支持AS2协议，无法直接与IdP集成进行身份验证。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS DataSync is mainly used to efficiently transfer data between different storage systems, but it does not support the AS2 protocol and cannot directly integrate with IdP for authentication.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon AppFlow 用于自动化 AWS 服务和 SaaS 应用程序之间的数据流，但它不支持 AS2 协议。此外，ECS 任务并不是处理 IdP 身份验证的最佳选择。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon AppFlow is used to automate data flow between AWS services and SaaS applications, but it does not support the AS2 protocol. Additionally, ECS tasks are not the best choice for handling IdP authentication.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Transfer Family 支持 AS2 协议，可用于安全数据传输。 Dapi AWS Lambda可以与公司的IdP集成进行身份认证。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Transfer Family supports the AS2 protocol and can be used for secure data transfer. Dapi AWS Lambda can be integrated with the company's IdP for identity authentication.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Storage Gateway 主要用于混合云存储解决方案，不支持 AS2 协议。此外，虽然 Amazon Cognito 可用于身份验证，但它不符合 AS2 协议的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Storage Gateway is mainly used for hybrid cloud storage solutions and does not support the AS2 protocol. Additionally, while Amazon Cognito can be used for authentication, it does not match the requirements of the AS2 protocol.

answer_text.zh: C. 使用 AWS Transfer Family 传输数据。创建用于 IdP 身份验证的 AWS Lambda 函数。
answer_text.en: C. Use AWS Transfer Family to transfer the data. Create an AWS Lambda function for IdP authentication.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (91%) 9%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Transfer Family 支持 AS2 协议，可用于安全数据传输。 Dapi AWS Lambda可以与公司的IdP集成进行身份认证。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (91%) 9%. Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Transfer Family supports the AS2 protocol and can be used for secure data transfer. Dapi AWS Lambda can be integrated with the company's IdP for identity authentication.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解决方案架构师正在 Amazon API Gateway 中设计 RESTAPI 以实现现金回报服务。该应用程序需要 1 GB 内存和 2 GB 存储空间用于其计算资源。应用程序将要求数据采用关系格式。哪种额外的 AWS 服务组合能够以最少的管理工作满足这些要求？ （选择两个。）
question_text.en: A solutions architect is designing a RESTAPI in Amazon API Gateway for a cash payback service. The application requires 1 GB of memory and 2 GB of storage for its computation resources. The application will require that the data is in a relational format. Which additional combination ofAWS services will meet these requirements with the LEAST administrative effort? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 亚马逊EC2
A.en: Amazon EC2

B.zh: AWS Lambda
B.en: AWS Lambda

C.zh: 亚马逊 RDS 亚马逊 RDS
C.en: Amazon RDS

D.zh: 亚马逊动态数据库
D.en: Amazon DynamoDB

E.zh: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes 服务 (Amazon EKS)

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon EC2可以提供计算资源和存储，但需要人工管理操作系统、数据库安装和维护，增加了管理复杂度，不符合“最小管理工作量”的要求。因此出现了错误。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon EC2 can provide computing resources and storage, but requires manual management of operating systems, database installation, and maintenance, which increases management complexity and does not meet the requirements of "minimum management effort." Hence the error.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Lambda 是一种无服务器计算服务，支持 API Gateway 集成，但 Lambda 函数的内存限制为 10 GB，并且不直接提供持久存储。虽然满足了计算需求，但无法满足存储需求。因此出现了错误。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports API Gateway integration, but the memory limit of Lambda functions is 10 GB and does not directly provide persistent storage. Although it meets the computing needs, it cannot meet the storage needs. Hence the error.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon RDS 是一种托管关系数据库服务，支持关系格式来存储数据，同时减少管理工作量。符合题目要求，因此正确。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service that supports relational formats for storing data with less management workload. It meets the question requirements and is therefore correct.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon DynamoDB 是托管 NoSQL 数据库服务，不支持关系数据存储格式，因此会出现错误。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon DynamoDB is a managed NoSQL database service and does not support relational data storage formats, hence the error.

E.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon EKS 可以运行容器化应用程序，但需要管理容器编排和存储，这增加了管理复杂性，不满足“最少管理工作量”的要求。因此出现了错误。
E.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon EKS can run containerized applications, but it needs to manage container orchestration and storage, which increases management complexity and does not meet the requirements of "minimum management effort." Hence the error.

answer_text.zh: B. AWS Lambda；C. 亚马逊 RDS 亚马逊 RDS
answer_text.en: B. AWS Lambda; C. Amazon RDS

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：BC (89%) 11%。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Lambda 是一种无服务器计算服务，支持 API Gateway 集成，但 Lambda 函数的内存限制为 10 GB，并且不直接提供持久存储。虽然满足了计算需求，但无法满足存储需求。因此出现了错误。；Amazon RDS 是一种托管关系数据库服务，支持关系格式来存储数据，同时减少管理工作量。符合题目要求，因此正确。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BC (89%) 11%. Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports API Gateway integration, but the memory limit of Lambda functions is 10 GB and does not directly provide persistent storage. Although it meets the computing needs, it cannot meet the storage needs. Hence the error.; Amazon RDS is a managed relational database service that supports relational formats for storing data with less management workload. It meets the question requirements and is therefore correct.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用 AWS Organizations 在多个 AWS 账户中运行工作负载。当公司创建标签时，标记策略会将部门标签添加到 AWS 资源。会计团队需要确定 Amazon EC2 消耗的支出。会计团队必须确定哪些部门负责成本，无论 AWS 账户如何。会计团队可以访问组织内所有 AWS 账户的 AWS Cost Explorer，并且需要访问 Cost Explorer 中的所有报告。哪种解决方案以最高效的方式满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company uses AWS Organizations to run workloads within multiple AWS accounts. A tagging policy adds department tags to AWS resources when the company creates tags. An accounting team needs to determine spending on Amazon EC2 consumption. The accounting team must determine which departments are responsible for the costs regardless ofAWS account. The accounting team has access to AWS Cost Explorer for all AWS accounts within the organization and needs to access all reports from Cost Explorer. Which solution meets these requirements in the MOST operationally efficient way?

A.zh: 从组织管理账户计费控制台中，激活名为“部门”的用户定义的成本分配标签。在 Cost Explorer 中创建一份成本报告，按标签名称分组，并按 EC2 进行过滤。
A.en: From the Organizations management account billing console, activate a user-defined cost allocation tag named department. Create one cost report in Cost Explorer grouping by tag name, and filter by EC2.

B.zh: 从组织管理账户计费控制台中，激活名为“部门”的 AWS 定义的成本分配标签。在 Cost Explorer 中创建一份成本报告，按标签名称分组，并按 EC2 进行过滤。
B.en: From the Organizations management account billing console, activate an AWS-defined cost allocation tag named department. Create one cost report in Cost Explorer grouping by tag name, and filter by EC2.

C.zh: 从组织成员账户计费控制台中，激活名为“部门”的用户定义的成本分配标签。在 Cost Explorer 中创建一份成本报告，按标签名称分组，并按 EC2 进行过滤。
C.en: From the Organizations member account billing console, activate a user-defined cost allocation tag named department. Create one cost report in Cost Explorer grouping by the tag name, and filter by EC2.

D.zh: 从组织成员账户计费控制台中，激活名为“部门”的 AWS 定义的成本分配标签。在 Cost Explorer 中创建一份成本报告，按标签名称分组，并按 EC2 进行过滤。
D.en: From the Organizations member account billing console, activate an AWS-defined cost allocation tag named department. Create one cost report in Cost Explorer grouping by tag name, and filter by EC2.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：管理账户可以集中管理所有会员账户的费用分配。正确：可以根据需求自定义自定义成本分配标签，例如“部门”。正确：Cost Explorer 支持按类型对资源类型（例如 EC2）进行分组和过滤。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: The management account can centrally manage the cost allocation of all member accounts. Correct: User-defined cost allocation labels can be customized according to requirements, such as "department". Correct: Cost Explorer supports grouping and filtering resource types (such as EC2) by type.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS 定义的成本分配标签由 AWS 预定义，没有自定义“部门”标签。正确：管理账户可以集中管理成本分配。正确：Cost Explorer 支持按类型对资源类型（例如 EC2）进行分组和过滤。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: The cost allocation label defined by AWS is predefined by AWS and has no custom "department" label. Correct: Management accounts can centrally manage cost allocations. Correct: Cost Explorer supports grouping and filtering resource types (such as EC2) by type.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：会员账户集中管理所有账户的费用分配，无需剥离。正确：可以根据需求自定义自定义成本分配标签，例如“部门”。正确：Cost Explorer 支持按类型对资源类型（例如 EC2）进行分组和过滤。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Member accounts centrally manage the cost allocation of all accounts without stripping. Correct: User-defined cost allocation labels can be customized according to requirements, such as "department". Correct: Cost Explorer supports grouping and filtering resource types (such as EC2) by type.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：会员账户集中管理所有账户的费用分配，无需剥离。错误：AWS 定义的成本分配标签是由 AWS 预定义的，并且没有自定义“部门”标签。正确：Cost Explorer 支持按类型对资源类型（例如 EC2）进行分组和过滤。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Member accounts centrally manage the cost allocation of all accounts without stripping. Error: The cost allocation label defined by AWS is predefined by AWS, and there is no custom "department" label. Correct: Cost Explorer supports grouping and filtering resource types (such as EC2) by type.

answer_text.zh: A. 从组织管理账户计费控制台中，激活名为“部门”的用户定义的成本分配标签。在 Cost Explorer 中创建一份成本报告，按标签名称分组，并按 EC2 进行过滤。
answer_text.en: A. From the Organizations management account billing console, activate a user-defined cost allocation tag named department. Create one cost report in Cost Explorer grouping by tag name, and filter by EC2.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：管理账户可以集中管理所有会员账户的费用分配。正确：可以根据需求自定义自定义成本分配标签，例如“部门”。正确：Cost Explorer 支持按类型对资源类型（例如 EC2）进行分组和过滤。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: The management account can centrally manage the cost allocation of all member accounts. Correct: User-defined cost allocation labels can be customized according to requirements, such as "department". Correct: Cost Explorer supports grouping and filtering resource types (such as EC2) by type.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司希望在其软件即服务 (SaaS) 应用程序 Salesforce 账户和 Amazon S3 之间安全地交换数据。公司必须使用 AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) 客户管理密钥 (CMK) 加密静态数据。公司还必须对传输中的数据进行加密。该公司已为 Salesforce 账户启用 API 访问。
question_text.en: A company wants to securely exchange data between its software as a service (SaaS) application Salesforce account and Amazon S3. The company must encrypt the data at rest by using AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) customer managed keys (CMKs). The company must also encrypt the data in transit. The company has enabled API access for the Salesforce account.

A.zh: 创建 AWS Lambda 函数以将数据从 Salesforce 安全地传输到 Amazon S3。
A.en: Create AWS Lambda functions to transfer the data securely from Salesforce to Amazon S3.

B.zh: 创建 AWS Step Functions 工作流程。定义将数据从 Salesforce 安全传输到 Amazon S3 的任务。
B.en: Create an AWS Step Functions workflow. Define the task to transfer the data securely from Salesforce to Amazon S3.

C.zh: 创建 Amazon AppFlow 流以将数据从 Salesforce 安全地传输到 Amazon S3。
C.en: Create Amazon AppFlow flows to transfer the data securely from Salesforce to Amazon S3.

D.zh: 为 Salesforce 创建自定义连接器，以将数据从 Salesforce 安全地传输到 Amazon S3。
D.en: Create a custom connector for Salesforce to transfer the data securely from Salesforce to Amazon S3.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然 AWS Lambda 可用于处理数据传输，但它需要额外的开发工作才能与 Salesforce 集成并确保数据在传输和存储过程中加密。这不是最直接的解决方案。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: While AWS Lambda can be used to handle data transfer, it requires additional development work to enable integration with Salesforce and ensure data is encrypted in transit and storage. This is not the most straightforward solution.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Step Functions 主要用于编排工作流程，不直接处理数据传输。它需要集成其他服务（例如Lambda）来实现数据传输，这增加了复杂性。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Step Functions is mainly used to orchestrate workflows and does not directly handle data transmission. It requires the integration of other services (such as Lambda) to implement data transfer, which increases complexity.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon AppFlow 是一项专门为安全地集成和传输数据而设计的服务。支持与Salesforce原生集成，在传输和存储过程中自动处理数据加密，满足问题需求。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon AppFlow is a service specifically designed to integrate and transfer data securely. It supports native integration with Salesforce and automatically handles data encryption during transmission and storage, meeting the question requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：创建自定义连接器需要额外的开发工作，并且可能无法直接满足数据加密要求。这不是最佳解决方案。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Creating a custom connector requires additional development work and may not directly meet data encryption requirements. This is not the optimal solution.

answer_text.zh: C. 创建 Amazon AppFlow 流以将数据从 Salesforce 安全地传输到 Amazon S3。
answer_text.en: C. Create Amazon AppFlow flows to transfer the data securely from Salesforce to Amazon S3.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon AppFlow 是一项专门为安全地集成和传输数据而设计的服务。支持与Salesforce原生集成，在传输和存储过程中自动处理数据加密，满足问题需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon AppFlow is a service specifically designed to integrate and transfer data securely. It supports native integration with Salesforce and automatically handles data encryption during transmission and storage, meeting the question requirements.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在单个 AWS 区域中开发移动游戏应用程序。该应用程序在 Auto Scaling 组中的多个 Amazon EC2 实例上运行。该公司将应用程序数据存储在 Amazon DynamoDB 中。该应用程序通过使用用户和服务器之间的 TCP 流量和 UDP 流量进行通信。该应用程序将在全球范围内使用。该公司希望确保所有用户的延迟尽可能最低。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is developing a mobile gaming app in a single AWS Region. The app runs on multiple Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group. The company stores the app data in Amazon DynamoDB. The app communicates by using TCP traffic and UDP traffic between the users and the servers. The application will be used globally. The company wants to ensure the lowest possible latency for all users. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Global Accelerator 创建加速器。在使用 Global Accelerator 集成并侦听 TCP 和 UDP 端口的加速器端点后面创建应用程序负载均衡器 (ALB)。更新 Auto Scaling 组以在 ALB 上注册实例。
A.en: Use AWS Global Accelerator to create an accelerator. Create an Application Load Balancer (ALB) behind an accelerator endpoint that uses Global Accelerator integration and listening on the TCP and UDP ports. Update the Auto Scaling group to register instances on the ALB.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Global Accelerator 创建加速器。在使用全局加速器集成并侦听 TCP 和 UDP 端口的加速器端点后面创建网络负载均衡器 (NLB)。更新 Auto Scaling 组以在 NLB 上注册实例。
B.en: Use AWS Global Accelerator to create an accelerator. Create a Network Load Balancer (NLB) behind an accelerator endpoint that uses Global Accelerator integration and listening on the TCP and UDP ports. Update the Auto Scaling group to register instances on the NLB.

C.zh: 创建 Amazon CloudFront 内容分发网络 (CDN) 终端节点。在端点后面创建网络负载均衡器 (NLB) 并侦听 TCP 和 UDP 端口。更新 Auto Scaling 组以在 NLB 上注册实例。更新 CloudFront 以使用 NLB 作为源。
C.en: Create an Amazon CloudFront content delivery network (CDN) endpoint. Create a Network Load Balancer (NLB) behind the endpoint and listening on the TCP and UDP ports. Update the Auto Scaling group to register instances on the NLB. Update CloudFront to use the NLB as the origin.

D.zh: 创建 Amazon CloudFront 内容分发网络 (CDN) 终端节点。在端点后面创建应用程序负载均衡器 (ALB) 并侦听 TCP 和 UDP 端口。更新 Auto Scaling 组以在 ALB 上注册实例。更新 CloudFront 以使用 ALB 作为源。
D.en: Create an Amazon CloudFront content delivery network (CDN) endpoint. Create an Application Load Balancer (ALB) behind the endpoint and listening on the TCP and UDP ports. Update the Auto Scaling group to register instances on the ALB. Update CloudFront to use the ALB as the origin.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用AWS Global Accelerator和ALB：ALB主要用于处理HTTP/HTTPS流量，不支持UDP流量。问题显然需要支持TCP和UDP流量，所以ALB不满足要求。这个选项是错误的。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Using AWS Global Accelerator and ALB: ALB is mainly used to process HTTP/HTTPS traffic and does not support UDP traffic. The question clearly requires support for TCP and UDP traffic, so ALB does not meet the requirements. This option is wrong.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：使用 AWS Global Accelerator 和 NLB：NLB 支持 TCP 和 UDP 流量并满足问题要求。 AWS Global Accelerator 可以优化全球用户的访问路径并减少延迟。这个选项是正确的。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Using AWS Global Accelerator and NLB: NLB supports TCP and UDP traffic and meets the question requirements. AWS Global Accelerator can optimize access paths for global users and reduce latency. This option is correct.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用CloudFront和NLB：CloudFront主要用于内容分发（CDN），不适合处理实时TCP和UDP流量。 NLB 支持 TCP 和 UDP 流量，但 CloudFront 无法直接优化实时通信的延迟。这个选项是错误的。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Using CloudFront and NLB: CloudFront is mainly used for content distribution (CDN) and is not suitable for handling real-time TCP and UDP traffic. NLB supports TCP and UDP traffic, but CloudFront cannot directly optimize latency for real-time communications. This option is wrong.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用CloudFront和ALB：ALB不支持UDP流量，无法满足问题要求。 CloudFront 不适合处理实时 TCP 和 UDP 流量。这个选项是错误的。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Using CloudFront and ALB: ALB does not support UDP traffic and cannot meet the question requirements. CloudFront is not suitable for handling real-time TCP and UDP traffic. This option is wrong.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 AWS Global Accelerator 创建加速器。在使用全局加速器集成并侦听 TCP 和 UDP 端口的加速器端点后面创建网络负载均衡器 (NLB)。更新 Auto Scaling 组以在 NLB 上注册实例。
answer_text.en: B. Use AWS Global Accelerator to create an accelerator. Create a Network Load Balancer (NLB) behind an accelerator endpoint that uses Global Accelerator integration and listening on the TCP and UDP ports. Update the Auto Scaling group to register instances on the NLB.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：使用 AWS Global Accelerator 和 NLB：NLB 支持 TCP 和 UDP 流量并满足问题要求。 AWS Global Accelerator 可以优化全球用户的访问路径并减少延迟。这个选项是正确的。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Using AWS Global Accelerator and NLB: NLB supports TCP and UDP traffic and meets the question requirements. AWS Global Accelerator can optimize access paths for global users and reduce latency. This option is correct.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司有一个处理客户订单的应用程序。该公司将该应用程序托管在 Amazon EC2 实例上，该实例将订单保存到 Amazon Aurora 数据库中。有时，当流量很高时，工作负载处理订单的速度不够快。解决方案架构师应该如何做才能尽快将订单可靠地写入数据库？
question_text.en: A company has an application that processes customer orders. The company hosts the application on an Amazon EC2 instance that saves the orders to an Amazon Aurora database. Occasionally when traffic is high the workload does not process orders fast enough. What should a solutions architect do to write the orders reliably to the database as quickly as possible?

A.zh: 当流量较高时增加 EC2 实例的实例大小。将订单写入 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)。将数据库端点订阅 SNS 主题。
A.en: Increase the instance size of the EC2 instance when traffic is high. Write orders to Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS). Subscribe the database endpoint to the SNS topic.

B.zh: 将订单写入 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。使用应用程序负载均衡器后面的 Auto Scaling 组中的 EC2 实例从 SQS 队列中读取数据并将订单处理到数据库中。
B.en: Write orders to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Use EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer to read from the SQS queue and process orders into the database.

C.zh: 将订单写入 Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS)。将数据库端点订阅 SNS 主题。使用 Application Load Balancer 后面的 Auto Scaling 组中的 EC2 实例来读取 SNS 主题。
C.en: Write orders to Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS). Subscribe the database endpoint to the SNS topic. Use EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer to read from the SNS topic.

D.zh: 当 EC2 实例达到 CPU 阈值限制时，将订单写入 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。使用应用程序负载均衡器后面的 Auto Scaling 组中的 EC2 实例的计划扩展来从 SQS 队列中读取数据并将订单处理到数据库中。
D.en: Write orders to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue when the EC2 instance reaches CPU threshold limits. Use scheduled scaling of EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer to read from the SQS queue and process orders into the database.

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：增加 EC2 实例大小并使用 Amazon SNS：错误。虽然增加实例大小可以提高单个实例的处理能力，但这不是一个可扩展的解决方案。此外，Amazon SNS 是一种发布/订阅服务，无法直接保证消息的顺序或可靠性，数据库终端节点也无法直接订阅 SNS 主题。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Increasing EC2 instance size and using Amazon SNS: Error. While increasing the instance size can increase the processing power of a single instance, it is not a scalable solution. In addition, Amazon SNS is a publish/subscribe service and cannot directly guarantee the order or reliability of messages, nor can database endpoints directly subscribe to SNS topics.

B.zh: 正確。技术原因：使用 Amazon SQS 和 Auto Scaling：正确。 Amazon SQS 是一种可靠的消息队列服务，可缓冲订单请求以确保消息不会丢失。使用 Auto Scaling 组和负载均衡器动态扩展 EC2 实例以处理高流量，确保订单处理的可靠性和效率。
B.en: Correct. Technical reason: Using Amazon SQS and Auto Scaling: Correct. Amazon SQS is a reliable message queue service that buffers order requests to ensure messages are not lost. Use Auto Scaling groups and load balancers to dynamically scale EC2 instances to handle high traffic, ensuring order processing reliability and efficiency.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用 Amazon SNS 和 Auto Scaling：错误。 Amazon SNS 是一种发布/订阅服务，不适合用作消息队列。它不保证消息的顺序或可靠性，并且数据库端点不能直接订阅SNS主题。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Using Amazon SNS and Auto Scaling: Error. Amazon SNS is a publish/subscribe service and is not suitable for use as a message queue. It does not guarantee the order or reliability of messages, and database endpoints cannot directly subscribe to SNS topics.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：使用 Amazon SQS 和计划扩展：错误。计划的扩展基于预定义的时间表，而不是实时流量变化。这种方法无法动态响应流量峰值，并且可能导致订单处理延迟。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Using Amazon SQS and Schedule Extension: Error. Planned scaling is based on predefined schedules rather than real-time traffic changes. This approach cannot dynamically respond to traffic spikes and can cause delays in order processing.

answer_text.zh: B. 将订单写入 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 队列。使用应用程序负载均衡器后面的 Auto Scaling 组中的 EC2 实例从 SQS 队列中读取数据并将订单处理到数据库中。
answer_text.en: B. Write orders to an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Use EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group behind an Application Load Balancer to read from the SQS queue and process orders into the database.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：B (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：使用 Amazon SQS 和 Auto Scaling：正确。 Amazon SQS 是一种可靠的消息队列服务，可缓冲订单请求以确保消息不会丢失。使用 Auto Scaling 组和负载均衡器动态扩展 EC2 实例以处理高流量，确保订单处理的可靠性和效率。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Using Amazon SQS and Auto Scaling: Correct. Amazon SQS is a reliable message queue service that buffers order requests to ensure messages are not lost. Use Auto Scaling groups and load balancers to dynamically scale EC2 instances to handle high traffic, ensuring order processing reliability and efficiency.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家物联网公司正在发布一款带有传感器的床垫，可以收集有关用户睡眠的数据。传感器将数据发送到 Amazon S3 存储桶。传感器每晚为每个床垫收集大约 2 MB 的数据。公司必须处理和汇总每个床垫的数据。结果需要尽快提供。数据处理需要 1 GB 内存，并将在 30 秒内完成。哪种解决方案能够最具成本效益地满足这些要求？
question_text.en: An IoT company is releasing a mattress that has sensors to collect data about a user’s sleep. The sensors will send data to an Amazon S3 bucket. The sensors collect approximately 2 MB of data every night for each mattress. The company must process and summarize the data for each mattress. The results need to be available as soon as possible. Data processing will require 1 GB of memory and will finish within 30 seconds. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 将 AWS Glue 与 Scala 作业结合使用
A.en: Use AWS Glue with a Scala job

B.zh: 将 Amazon EMR 与 Apache Spark 脚本结合使用
B.en: Use Amazon EMR with an Apache Spark script

C.zh: 将 AWS Lambda 与 Python 脚本结合使用
C.en: Use AWS Lambda with a Python script

D.zh: 将 AWS Glue 与 PySpark 作业结合使用
D.en: Use AWS Glue with a PySpark job

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：AWS Glue 是一种托管 ETL 服务，适合处理大规模数据转换任务，但启动时间较长（通常为几分钟），不适合需要快速处理的任务。此外，使用 Scala 可能会增加开发复杂性，但该问题不需要特定的语言。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: AWS Glue is a managed ETL service that is suitable for handling large-scale data transformation tasks, but it has a long startup time (usually a few minutes) and is not suitable for tasks that require fast processing. Additionally, using Scala may increase development complexity, but the question does not require a specific language.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：Amazon EMR 是一种托管 Hadoop 和 Spark 集群服务，适用于大规模分布式数据处理任务。然而，由于数据量为2MB，处理时间要求为30秒，EMR的成本和复杂性太高，不符合“最具成本效益”的标准。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Amazon EMR is a managed Hadoop and Spark cluster service suitable for large-scale distributed data processing tasks. However, with a data volume of 2 MB and a processing time requirement of 30 seconds, the cost and complexity of EMR were too high to qualify as "most cost-effective."

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：AWS Lambda 是一种无服务器计算服务，支持 Python 脚本，可以快速启动和处理小规模数据任务。 Lambda 的最大内存限制为 10 GB，完全满足 1 GB 内存要求，其执行时间限制为 15 分钟，远远超过 30 秒的要求。对于 2 MB 的数据，Lambda 的成本非常低，并且不需要配置或管理基础设施。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports Python scripts and can quickly start and process small-scale data tasks. Lambda's maximum memory limit is 10 GB, which fully meets the 1 GB memory requirement, and its execution time is limited to 15 minutes, far exceeding the 30 second requirement. For 2 MB of data, Lambda is very low cost and requires no provisioning or management infrastructure.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然 PySpark 是流行的分布式数据处理桶框架，但 AWS Glue 启动时间较长，不适合需要快速处理的任务。此外，Glue 通常比 Lambda 更昂贵，尤其是对于小规模数据处理任务。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although PySpark is a popular distributed data processing bucket framework, AWS Glue has a long startup time and is not suitable for tasks that require fast processing. Additionally, Glue is generally more expensive than Lambda, especially for small-scale data processing tasks.

answer_text.zh: C. 将 AWS Lambda 与 Python 脚本结合使用
answer_text.en: C. Use AWS Lambda with a Python script

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：C (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：AWS Lambda 是一种无服务器计算服务，支持 Python 脚本，可以快速启动和处理小规模数据任务。 Lambda 的最大内存限制为 10 GB，完全满足 1 GB 内存要求，其执行时间限制为 15 分钟，远远超过 30 秒的要求。对于 2 MB 的数据，Lambda 的成本非常低，并且不需要配置或管理基础设施。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports Python scripts and can quickly start and process small-scale data tasks. Lambda's maximum memory limit is 10 GB, which fully meets the 1 GB memory requirement, and its execution time is limited to 15 minutes, far exceeding the 30 second requirement. For 2 MB of data, Lambda is very low cost and requires no provisioning or management infrastructure.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司托管一个在线购物应用程序，该应用程序将所有订单存储在 Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL 单可用区数据库实例中。管理层希望消除单点故障，并要求解决方案架构师推荐一种方法来最大限度地减少数据库停机时间，而无需对应用程序代码进行任何更改。哪种解决方案满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company hosts an online shopping application that stores all orders in an Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL Single-AZ DB instance. Management wants to eliminate single points of failure and has asked a solutions architect to recommend an approach to minimize database downtime without requiring any changes to the application code. Which solution meets these requirements?

A.zh: 通过修改数据库实例并指定多可用区选项，将现有数据库实例转换为多可用区部署。
A.en: Convert the existing database instance to a Multi-AZ deployment by modifying the database instance and specifying the Multi-AZ option.

B.zh: 创建新的 RDS 多可用区部署。拍摄当前 RDS 实例的快照并使用该快照恢复新的多可用区部署。
B.en: Create a new RDS Multi-AZ deployment. Take a snapshot of the current RDS instance and restore the new Multi-AZ deployment with the snapshot.

C.zh: 在另一个可用区中创建 PostgreSQL 数据库的只读副本。使用 Amazon Route 53 加权记录集在数据库之间分配请求。
C.en: Create a read-only replica of the PostgreSQL database in another Availability Zone. Use Amazon Route 53 weighted record sets to distribute requests across the databases.

D.zh: 将 RDS for PostgreSQL 数据库放置在 Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling 组中，组大小至少为 2。使用 Amazon Route 53 加权记录集跨实例分配请求。
D.en: Place the RDS for PostgreSQL database in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group with a minimum group size of two. Use Amazon Route 53 weighted record sets to distribute requests across instances.

A.zh: 正確。技术原因：Amazon RDS 支持通过修改现有数据库实例来启用多可用区部署。这种方法可以最大程度地减少停机时间，并且无需更改应用程序代码。满足问题要求：消除单点故障，最大限度地减少数据库停机时间，并且不需要更改应用程序代码。
A.en: Correct. Technical reason: Amazon RDS supports enabling Multi-AZ deployments by modifying existing database instances. This approach minimizes downtime and requires no changes to application code. Meets the question requirements: Eliminates single points of failure, minimizes database downtime, and requires no changes to application code.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然可以通过快照恢复到新的多可用区部署，但此过程会导致大量停机，因为需要创建新实例并恢复数据。不满足问题要求：数据库停机时间无法最小化。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: While it is possible to restore to a new Multi-AZ deployment via snapshots, this process results in significant downtime as new instances need to be created and data restored. Does not meet the question requirements: Database downtime cannot be minimized.

C.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：只读副本不能用于写操作，因此无法完全消除单点故障。不满足题目要求：无法解决主数据库的高可用问题。
C.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Read replicas cannot be used for write operations, so single points of failure cannot be completely eliminated. Does not meet the question requirements: Unable to solve the high availability problem of the main database.

D.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：RDS 数据库无法直接放置在 EC2 Auto Scaling 组中。此选项与 RDS 的工作方式不一致。不满足题目要求：无法实现RDS的高可用。
D.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: RDS databases cannot be placed directly in EC2 Auto Scaling groups. This option is inconsistent with how RDS works. Does not meet the question requirements: High availability of RDS cannot be achieved.

answer_text.zh: A. 通过修改数据库实例并指定多可用区选项，将现有数据库实例转换为多可用区部署。
answer_text.en: A. Convert the existing database instance to a Multi-AZ deployment by modifying the database instance and specifying the Multi-AZ option.

discussion.zh: 社区投票原始结果：A (100%)。热门讨论与考点摘要：Amazon RDS 支持通过修改现有数据库实例来启用多可用区部署。这种方法可以最大程度地减少停机时间，并且无需更改应用程序代码。满足问题要求：消除单点故障，最大限度地减少数据库停机时间，并且不需要更改应用程序代码。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Amazon RDS supports enabling Multi-AZ deployments by modifying existing database instances. This approach minimizes downtime and requires no changes to application code. Meets the question requirements: Eliminates single points of failure, minimizes database downtime, and requires no changes to application code.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在开发一款应用程序来支持客户需求。该公司希望将该应用程序部署在同一可用区内的多个基于 Amazon EC2 Nitro 的实例上。该公司还希望使应用程序能够同时写入多个基于 EC2 Nitro 的实例中的多个块存储卷，以实现更高的应用程序可用性。哪种解决方案可以满足这些要求？
question_text.en: A company is developing an application to support customer demands. The company wants to deploy the application on multiple Amazon EC2 Nitro-based instances within the same Availability Zone. The company also wants to give the application the ability to write to multiple block storage volumes in multiple EC2 Nitro-based instances simultaneously to achieve higher application availability. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 将通用 SSD (gp3) EBS 卷与 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 多重附加结合使用
A.en: Use General Purpose SSD (gp3) EBS volumes with Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) Multi-Attach

B.zh: 将吞吐量优化 HDD (st1) EBS 卷与 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 多重附加结合使用
B.en: Use Throughput Optimized HDD (st1) EBS volumes with Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) Multi-Attach

C.zh: 将预配置 IOPS SSD (io2) EBS 卷与 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 多重附加结合使用
C.en: Use Provisioned IOPS SSD (io2) EBS volumes with Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) Multi-Attach

D.zh: 将通用 SSD (gp2) EBS 卷与 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 多重附加结合使用
D.en: Use General Purpose SSD (gp2) EBS volumes with Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) Multi-Attach

A.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：虽然gp3是一个通用SSD卷，提供高性能和低成本，但它不支持EBS Multi-Attach功能。
A.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: Although gp3 is a general-purpose SSD volume that provides high performance and low cost, it does not support the EBS Multi-Attach function.

B.zh: 錯誤。技术原因：st1 是吞吐量优化的 HDD 卷，适合大规模顺序读写工作负载，但不支持 EBS Multi-Attach 功能。
B.en: Incorrect. Technical reason: st1 is a throughput-optimized HDD volume suitable for large-scale sequential read and write workloads, but it does not support the EBS Multi-Attach feature.

C.zh: 正確。技术原因：io2是一款高性能SSD卷，支持EBS Multi-Attach功能，允许多个EC2实例同时挂载同一个卷，满足题目要求的高可用和多实例写入能力。
C.en: Correct. Technical reason: io2 is a high-performance SSD volume that supports the EBS Multi-Attach function, allowing multiple EC2 instances to mount the same volume at the same time, meeting the high availability and multi-instance writing capabilities required by the question.
